message:
🌱 Add cert-manager: Ingress routes for challenges created with pathType: mission (#1308)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cert-manager-6805-ingress-routes-for-challenges-created-with-pathtype-implementa",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cert-manager: Ingress routes for challenges created with pathType: ImplementationSpecific not working with Cilium",
+    "description": "Ingress routes for challenges created with pathType: ImplementationSpecific not working with Cilium. This issue affects 18+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify cert-manager troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your cert-manager deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review cert-manager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager\n```\nBasically the opposite of #4371\n\nNot sure if BUG or Feature Request\n\nWith Cilium and l2announcer one need to use the edit inline feature, because otherwise it will create new LoadBalancers with new IPs."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Ingress routes for challenges created with pathType:…",
+        "description": "Fixes cert-manager/cert-manager#6805\n\n### Pull Request Motivation\n\ncert-manager's acme http challenge accidentally uses Ingress pathType `ImplementationSpecific`, when it should use the more specific and secure `Exact` one. \n\nKubernetes Ingress controllers vary in implementation of the `ImplementationSpecific` pathType, which will make the deterministic behaviour of cert-manager unreliable depending on the ingress controller that is used. Eg. Cilium Ingress.\n\n### Kind\r\n\n### Release Note\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Ingress routes for challenges created with… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Fixes cert-manager/cert-manager#6805\n\n### Pull Request Motivation\n\ncert-manager's acme http challenge accidentally uses Ingress pathType `ImplementationSpecific`, when it should use the more specific and secure `Exact` one.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "cert-manager",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "cert-manager"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Ingress"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "graduated",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/issues/6805",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/pull/7767"
+    },
+    "reactions": 18,
+    "comments": 19,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with cert-manager installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
